Politics.headline: U.S. strikes Iran in response to attacks on warships, military says
Politics.detail: The burst of violence cast fresh doubt on efforts by Washington and Tehran to reach a negotiated settlement that would end hostilities.

Politics.headline: Tennessee approves new congressional map dissolving majority Black district
Politics.detail: Tennessee Republicans approved a measure to overturn the state's ban on mid-decade redistricting.

Politics.headline: U.S. trade court rules against Trump's 10% tariffs
Politics.detail: The tariffs were put in place in February, days after the Supreme Court struck down President Trump's previous round of sweeping "Liberation Day" tariffs.

Politics.headline: Republican lawmakers in Tennessee approved a new U.S. House map Thursday that carves up a majority-Black district in Memphis
Politics.detail: This reshapes it to the GOP's advantage as part of President Donald Trump's strategy to hold on to a slim majority.

Politics.headline: Four killed in post-election violence in India's West Bengal
Politics.detail: Unrest grips the key eastern state after Modi's Hindu nationalist BJP wins the state election for the first time.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil prices sink and stocks leap worldwide on hopes for a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz
Finance & Economy.detail: The price for a barrel of Brent fell below $102 Wednesday, leading to a surge in stock markets worldwide, with the S&P 500 climbing 1.5% and reaching another record.

Finance & Economy.headline: Another major setback for Trump's global tariff ambitions; his10% duty ruled unlawful
Finance & Economy.detail: A recent ruling has dealt a blow to Trump's global tariff plans, deeming his 10% duty unlawful, adding uncertainty to international trade relations.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Futures Edge Lower on Hormuz Clashes
Finance & Economy.detail: US stock futures experienced a slight decrease as renewed tensions between the US and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz elevated geopolitical concerns, overshadowing anticipation for the upcoming April jobs report.

Finance & Economy.headline: Saudi Arabia posts $33.5bn budget deficit amid drop in oil sales
Finance & Economy.detail: Saudi Arabia has reported a budget deficit of $33.5 billion due to a decrease in oil sales, impacting the nation's financial outlook.

Finance & Economy.headline: Productivity growth has been slower, with AI's impact yet to come
Finance & Economy.detail: Despite the buzz around artificial intelligence, recent data indicates that productivity growth has been sluggish, suggesting that the transformative impact of AI on the economy is still on the horizon.

Technology.headline: FCC Chair: Starlink Isn't Enough. We Need at Least 3 Satellite-to-Phone Services.
Technology.detail: The FCC chair believes that multiple satellite-to-phone services are needed, implying that Starlink alone is insufficient to meet the demand and ensure competition in the market. This perspective highlights the importance of redundancy and diverse options for satellite-based communication services.

Technology.headline: Global Tech Care Company Asurion Opens Cebu Site, Creating 2,000 Jobs
Technology.detail: Asurion, a global tech care company, has opened a new customer solutions center in Cebu City, Philippines, which is expected to create approximately 2,000 local jobs over the next two years. This expansion signifies Asurion's commitment to providing reliable tech support at scale and its confidence in the Philippines as a critical part of its global operations.

Technology.headline: Nvidia to invest up to $2.1 billion in IREN as part of AI data center deal
Technology.detail: Nvidia plans to invest up to $2.1 billion in data center operator IREN to deploy up to 5 gigawatts of infrastructure, addressing the increasing demand for computing power driven by AI. This partnership aims to accelerate the deployment of large-scale AI factories, combining Nvidia's factory architecture with IREN's infrastructure operations.

Technology.headline: Tech is turning increasingly to religion in a quest to create ethical AI
Technology.detail: As concerns grow about AI's rapid integration into society, tech companies are increasingly seeking guidance from faith leaders on shaping the technology, marking a shift from Silicon Valley's skepticism towards organized religion. Companies like Anthropic and OpenAI participated in the inaugural “Faith-AI Covenant” roundtable to discuss integrating morality and ethics into AI development.

Technology.headline: Europe's AI translation industry told it risks reputation by partnering with US firms
Technology.detail: The European AI translation industry is facing warnings that partnering with US firms could jeopardize their reputation. This concern likely stems from differing data privacy standards, ethical considerations, and potential risks associated with data security and sovereignty.

Science.headline: Eating Eggs Could Cut Alzheimer's Risk by 27%
Science.detail: Researchers found that people 65 and older who eat eggs regularly have a significantly lower risk of developing Alzheimer's disease. Daily or near-daily egg consumption may offer significant brain health benefits.

Science.headline: A grapefruit-sized quantum device mapped Earth’s magnetic field from space
Science.detail: A compact quantum device, about the size of a grapefruit, has successfully mapped Earth's magnetic field from space. This innovative technology could lead to more precise navigation and a better understanding of our planet.

Science.headline: Garlic Compound May Hold Clue to Slowing Muscle Aging
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered that a compound found in garlic may offer a way to combat muscle aging. The research suggests potential therapeutic applications for maintaining muscle health in older adults.

Science.headline: New water battery could last until the 24th century — and it can be safely discarded in the environment
Science.detail: A newly developed water battery boasts a potential lifespan extending into the 24th century and can be safely discarded in the environment. This innovative battery design offers a sustainable and long-lasting energy storage solution.

Science.headline: Male Birth Control Breakthrough: Scientists Find Way To Turn Sperm Production Off and Back On
Science.detail: Scientists have achieved a breakthrough in male birth control by discovering a method to reversibly switch sperm production on and off. This development could lead to new contraceptive options for men.

Health.headline: Experts Raise Concerns Over Use of ECT in Australia
Health.detail: Experts are voicing concerns regarding the use of electroconvulsive therapy (ECT) in Australia.

Health.headline: Two Britons evacuated from hantavirus-hit ship 'improving' in hospital
Health.detail: Two British nationals who were evacuated from a cruise ship affected by a hantavirus outbreak are reportedly showing signs of improvement in the hospital.

Health.headline: Global race under way to trace passengers who left hantavirus ship before outbreak confirmed
Health.detail: A global effort is underway to trace passengers who disembarked from a cruise ship before a hantavirus outbreak was confirmed on board.

Health.headline: Rapid Sepsis Testing Can Save Lives and Curb Costs
Health.detail: Implementing rapid sepsis testing can potentially save lives and reduce healthcare costs associated with the condition.

Health.headline: Is the Global Fight Against Malaria Losing Ground?
Health.detail: There are concerns that the global fight against malaria may be losing momentum.

Sports.headline: Real Madrid's Valverde suffers head injury in altercation with teammate Tchouaméni
Sports.detail: Real Madrid's Federico Valverde reportedly suffered a head injury following an altercation with teammate Aurelien Tchouameni. The details surrounding the incident remain unclear, but it has been confirmed that Valverde sustained an injury.

Sports.headline: Hornets give coach Charles Lee a contract extension after 25-win improvement
Sports.detail: The Charlotte Hornets have extended coach Charles Lee's contract after he oversaw a 25-win improvement in the team's performance. This extension signifies the organization's confidence in Lee's leadership and coaching abilities.

Sports.headline: Yanks' Jasson Domínguez sprains shoulder crashing into wall, leading to Spencer Jones call-up
Sports.detail: New York Yankees' Jasson Domínguez suffered a shoulder sprain after crashing into a wall while making a catch. As a result, Spencer Jones has been called up to replace him on the roster.

Sports.headline: Liberty move to top of WNBA title odds after adding Satou Sabally with champion Aces close behind
Sports.detail: Following the acquisition of Satou Sabally, the New York Liberty have become the favorites to win the WNBA title, closely followed by the defending champion Las Vegas Aces. This move significantly alters the landscape of the WNBA title race.

Sports.headline: McCarthy hits a grand slam as the Rockies beat the Mets 6-2 to end a 6-game skid
Sports.detail: Sean McCarthy hit a grand slam helping the Colorado Rockies beat the New York Mets 6-2, ending their six-game losing streak. The grand slam was a key factor in the Rockies' victory.

Entertainment.headline: Shakira teases new song for the FIFA World Cup 2026 with Afrobeats star Burna Boy called 'Dai Dai'
Entertainment.detail: The Colombian singer Shakira has shared a minute-long teaser clip of a new song on social media on Thursday, which is set to be the official song for the FIFA World Cup 2026.

Entertainment.headline: Olivia Rodrigo's 'Unraveled' Tour 2026: Ticket Prices, Presale Date & More
Entertainment.detail: Find all the information you need on tickets and dates for Olivia Rodrigo's 'Unraveled' Tour 2026.

Entertainment.headline: Rapper Kodak Black due in court in Florida on felony drug charge
Entertainment.detail: Rapper Kodak Black was arrested in Florida on felony MDMA trafficking charge and pleads not guilty.

Entertainment.headline: RHOBH's PK Claims Dorit Spent $1 Million on Luxury Goods Instead of Mortgage
Entertainment.detail: PK, from The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills, claims that Dorit spent $1 million on luxury goods instead of paying the mortgage.

Entertainment.headline: Lea Michele Cleared By Doctor to Return to Broadway's 'Chess' After Missing Shows Due to Illness
Entertainment.detail: Lea Michele has been cleared by her doctor to return to Broadway's 'Chess' after missing shows due to illness.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man gets life in prison for firebombing pro-Israel demonstrators in Colorado
Crime & Justice.detail: A man was sentenced to life in prison for firebombing pro-Israel demonstrators in Colorado in June 2025, in an attack that targeted supporters of Israeli captives in Gaza. Advocates say the family of the assailant was unjustly targeted.

Crime & Justice.headline: Rapper Kodak Black due in court in Florida on felony drug charge
Crime & Justice.detail: Rapper Kodak Black was arrested in Florida on a felony MDMA trafficking charge and pleaded not guilty. He is due in court.

Crime & Justice.headline: Authorities search home linked to 1996 killing of college student Kristin Smart
Crime & Justice.detail: Authorities searched a home linked to the 1996 killing of college student Kristin Smart.

Crime & Justice.headline: Woman aged 28 pretended to be 16 and enrolled at New York high school before arrest
Crime & Justice.detail: A 28-year-old woman was arrested after pretending to be 16 years old and enrolling at a New York high school.

Crime & Justice.headline: Military Artificial Intelligence (military AI) and international humanitarian law
Crime & Justice.detail: A podcast looks into the new role of Artificial intelligence in military targeting and explains what it means for international humanitarian law. It is seen as “the ultimate technology”.

Education.headline: Maharashtra Class 10 SSC Result 2026 On May 8 At 1 PM; Here's How To Check
Education.detail: The Maharashtra State Board of Secondary and Higher Secondary Education (MSBSHSE) will declare the Class 10 (SSC) results on May 8, 2026, at 1 PM, and students can check their results on the official websites.

Education.headline: School Assembly News Headlines (May 8): Top National, World, Business, Sports News
Education.detail: A compilation of top news headlines suitable for school assembly, covering national, world, business, and sports events, is released daily.

Education.headline: Tamil Nadu Class 12 results to be declared on May 8
Education.detail: The Tamil Nadu Class 12 results are scheduled to be declared on May 8, providing students with their academic performance assessment.

Education.headline: New Texas A&M president confirmed as university seeks stability
Education.detail: Regents have unanimously appointed Susan Ballabina as president of Texas A&M University, entrusting her with leadership amidst ongoing fallout from the previous president's resignation.

Education.headline: World's Education Leaders to Convene in Singapore for Better Future Forum 2026
Education.detail: The Better Future Forum, hosted by the Global Institute for Shaping a Better Future, will take place in Singapore from May 12-14, 2026, gathering 200 global education leaders to discuss holistic student development in the face of AI, geopolitical conflict, and inequality.

Lifestyle.headline: Malaika Arora Shuts Down Cosmetic Surgery Rumours: "Don't Need That Right Now"
Lifestyle.detail: Malaika Arora addresses rumours about cosmetic surgery, stating she doesn't feel the need for it currently.

Lifestyle.headline: Small-group meet-ups, shared interests: How young women in Singapore are finding new social circles
Lifestyle.detail: Female-led social groups in Singapore are offering young women low-pressure, activity-based ways to meet and build friendships, from pilates to supper clubs.

Lifestyle.headline: Nipper, stay! The future of a beloved dog statue on a New York warehouse is up in the air
Lifestyle.detail: The fate of the iconic Nipper dog statue atop a New York warehouse is uncertain.

Lifestyle.headline: A Two-Night Culinary Sonata: Megu Arrives at The Leela Hyderabad With... Raen
Lifestyle.detail: The Leela Hyderabad hosts Megu, presenting a curated showcase of Modern Japanese flavours through immersive, multi-course dining.

Lifestyle.headline: From self-care to sweet treats: Mother's Day 2026 gift ideas
Lifestyle.detail: This article provides gift ideas for Mother's Day 2026, including self-care items and sweet treats.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL - Gas up and run
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Since fuel prices began skyrocketing, several cases have been reported of motorists filling their vehicles' tanks at gas stations, and then driving away without paying.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Forcing Inclusive Finance on Banks Risks Harmful Side Effects
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial board discusses President Lee Jae-myung's push for 'inclusive finance' and the potential risks of excessive government pressure on the financial sector. They highlight the need for careful review and design to avoid market distortions.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: How federal regulations choke U.S. science
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Washington Post Editorial Board discusses how investment in research is a good use of tax dollars, but bureaucracy is reducing the return on investment.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Whoever wins today's elections, democracy is the loser under first past the post
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Polly Toynbee writes that the outdated first past the post system is destroying trust in politics. She argues it's time for Labour to follow Wales and Scotland's example and introduce PR in the UK.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: General education and the struggle between the state and intellectuals
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece discussing the relationship between general education, the state, and intellectuals.

World.headline: US Trade Court Rules Against Trump's 10% Global Tariffs
World.detail: The U.S. Court of International Trade ruled that President Donald Trump's global tariff is illegal, invalidating his 10 percent tariffs on most U.S. imports because Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 allows tariffs only when there are "large and serious balance-of-payment deficits".

World.headline: Iran Says US Violated Ceasefire, Targeted Ships and Civilian Areas
World.detail: Iran's military command reported that the U.S. army attacked two Iranian vessels near the Strait of Hormuz and conducted airstrikes on civilian areas in Hormozgan province and Qeshm Island, calling the attacks a violation of the ceasefire. However, Trump downplayed the exchange as a mere "love tap".

World.headline: Health Officials Downplay Pandemic Risk from Cruise Hantavirus Outbreak
World.detail: World Health Organization officials stated that the hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is not likely to trigger a pandemic, with Maria Van Kerkhove emphasizing that it is not a new virus and the situation is different from the early stages of COVID-19. The Dutch-flagged Hondius is heading to the Canary Islands after evacuating three people in Cape Verde.

World.headline: NZ Seeks to Expand Navy, Citing Rising Global Tensions
World.detail: New Zealand is considering expanding its frigate fleet beyond the current two vessels, with Defence Minister Chris Penk stating that all options are on the table, including purchasing new warships from Japan or the UK, to replace its aging Anzac-class frigates. The UK's Type 31 and Japan's Mogami-class warships are under consideration.

World.headline: Child Marriage Restraint Bill Passed in Pakistan's Punjab Assembly
World.detail: Christian Solidarity Worldwide welcomed the passing of the Child Marriage Restraint Bill 2026 by the Punjab Assembly in Pakistan, which raises the minimum legal age for marriage for girls from 16 to 18 and declares underage marriage a non-bailable offense, aiming to prevent abduction and forced marriage of Christian and Hindu girls. The bill was presented by Punjab Parliamentary Affairs Minister Mujtaba Shujaur Rehman.

Zimbabwe.headline: 17 dead, 33 injured as Malawi-bound coach crashes on Harare–Nyamapanda Highway, State of disaster declared
Zimbabwe.detail: A Malawi-bound bus crashed on the Harare-Nyamapanda Highway, resulting in 17 fatalities and 33 injuries, prompting a state of disaster declaration.

Zimbabwe.headline: WestRidge head, wife in court over alleged shareholding fraud
Zimbabwe.detail: The head of WestRidge school and his wife are facing legal action over allegations of fraudulent shareholding practices.

Zimbabwe.headline: EU unveils €7.8 million boost for Zimbabwe horticulture sector
Zimbabwe.detail: The European Union has announced a €7.8 million investment to support the horticulture sector in Zimbabwe.

Zimbabwe.headline: India to host Zimbabwe for maiden women's bilateral series
Zimbabwe.detail: India will host Zimbabwe for their first-ever women's bilateral series in cricket.

Zimbabwe.headline: Government declares Monavale wetland 'no-go area' after residents' protest.
Zimbabwe.detail: Following protests by residents, the government has declared the Monavale wetland a protected area.
